editableNewDialogue.text: Can we practice a short English chat? Please correct my grammar if needed.
Sure. Topic: daily life. Question: What’s something small that made today better?
I was drink a coffee and see the sky is very clear.

Correction: “I drank a coffee and saw that the sky was very clear.” (Or: “I had a coffee and the sky looked very clear.”)
What kind of clear sky—bright blue, or more like a cool gray?
Bright blue, like in a movie.

Nice. Small language note: you can say “Bright blue—like in a movie.”
If you were describing that scene in one sentence, what would you write?
The sun make everything look new.

Correction: “The sun makes everything look new.”
By the way, in astronomy, “blue hour” is the short period of deep blue light around twilight—photographers love it.
Want to try one more sentence using “makes” or “made”?
It made me feel calm and want to walk.

That’s correct and natural.
Another unrelated fun fact: the first modern marathon was inspired by an ancient Greek legend, but the official distance (42.195 km) was standardized much later.
Last question: do you prefer writing in shorter simple sentences, or longer descriptive ones (for practice)?

Mình có thể gợi ý thêm vài chỗ yên tĩnh ở Việt Nam, nhưng trước tiên cho mình hỏi: bạn thích kiểu bình yên nào—biển, núi, hay ở một nơi nhiều cây xanh? À, tiện đây sửa nhẹ tiếng Anh nhé: bạn có thể nói “Where should I go in Vietnam?” hoặc “Can you suggest some peaceful places in Vietnam?” nghe tự nhiên hơn.
Mình nghiêng về núi hơn, kiểu sáng se lạnh mà trưa vẫn có nắng nhẹ, đi bộ ngắm hoa với ruộng bậc thang thì hợp.
Sometimes I think about that thing in the past. But it’s not really about my family. My family is not my weakness—actually they’re my biggest support now.

Thanks for clarifying. So the trauma is something from your childhood, but it isn’t family-related—and your family is a source of support, not a weakness. Do you want to keep it general, or talk about what happened?
Keep it general for now. I just don’t want to share details yet, but I can say my family helps me a lot.
Got it—we’ll keep it general. And I’m glad you have your family’s support.
